meaning: 马兰比季河[澳大利亚东南部](墨累河的重要支流)
examples: AFTER a ten-year drought, farmers along the Murrumbidgee River now face ruin from a devastating flood.
Without irrigation, no crops would grow in the dry plains of south-western New south Wales, where the Murrumbidgee meets the Murray.
Without irrigation, no crops would grow in the dry plains of south-western New South Wales, where the Murrumbidgee meets the Murray.
In 1912 pioneers diverted the Murrumbidgee's waters into canals to create one of the biggest irrigation projects in Australia, the world's second-driest continent after Antarctica.
The Murrumbidgee is a big tributary of the Murray.
